description_long: This is a modern commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage, swept-back wings, and two large turbofan engines mounted under the wings. The wings are low-mounted with noticeable upward-curved winglets at the tips, enhancing aerodynamic efficiency. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage, with a multi-panel windshield and several passenger windows running along the sides. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and stowed within the fuselage and wings. The exterior is smooth and painted in a uniform light color, typical of commercial aircraft.
description: A sleek, modern commercial jet airliner with swept-back low wings, underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. The fuselage is streamlined and painted in a light color.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and both underwing engines with the full wingspan and upward-curved winglets visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, cockpit placement, passenger windows, and the low-mounted swept wings with visible engines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile similar to view 1, showing the other side of the fuselage, wings, and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and upward-curved winglets at the tips.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle (not visible, inferred from typical design)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with a multi-panel windshield
